html:
[203]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 未半年 - Literally means 'not half a year,' indicating a period of less than six months.
  • 题升 - Refers to the process of promotion or appointment by imperial decree.
  • 通判 - A title of a local administrative official, equivalent to a deputy governor.
  • 同知 - A title of a local administrative official, equivalent to an associate governor.
  • 南安府知府 - A title of the governor of Nan'an Prefecture.
  • 阿公调任河南 - Indicates that the person referred to as '阿公' was transferred to Henan.
  • 华亦乞养 - Suggests that '华' also requested to return home to care for his parents.
  • 满载而归 - A phrase meaning to return home with great success or wealth.
  • 赏张荣二千金 - Indicates that Zhang Rong was rewarded with 2,000 taels of gold.
  • 张亦小康 - Suggests that Zhang Rong also achieved a comfortable life.
  • 傅四爷，吏部司官中之能员也 - A formal introduction of Fu Siye as a capable official in the Ministry of Personnel.
  • 果毅公讷亲掌吏部时 - Indicates that Guoyi Gong Neqin was in charge of the Ministry of Personnel.
  • 凡众司官说堂有不能了之事 - Refers to all officials in the Ministry of Personnel who could not resolve certain matters.
  • 唤傅来，数言而决 - Means that Fu Siye was called in, and the matter was resolved in a few words.
  • 讷甚重之 - Indicates that Neqin highly valued Fu Siye.
  • 故事 - A term used to introduce a tradition or custom.
  • 保举郎中 - Refers to the practice of recommending candidates for the position of Langzhong (a high-ranking official).
  • 一正一副 - Indicates that there were two types of recommendations: one for the main position and one for the deputy position.
  • 有户部郎中缺出 - Indicates that a vacancy for a Langzhong in the Ministry of Revenue occurred.
  • 光明殿 - Refers to the Bright Hall, a place where the emperor would meet officials.
  • 傅乍入殿门即跪 - Describes Fu Siye bowing upon entering the hall.
  • 上觉其呆 - Indicates that the emperor found Fu Siye to be foolish.
  • 用副荐者 - Means that the deputy recommendation was used instead.
  • 逾年 - Indicates a period of more than a year.
  • 吏部郎中缺出 - Indicates that a vacancy for a Langzhong in the Ministry of Personnel occurred.
  • 傅入殿门又即跪 - Describes Fu Siye bowing upon entering the hall again.
  • 上不悦 - Indicates that the emperor was displeased.
  • 如此等昏人，如何保举 - A rhetorical question expressing the emperor's dissatisfaction with Fu Siye's behavior.
  • 傅某办事甚好，是以屡荐之 - Indicates that Fu Siye was recommended multiple times due to his good performance.
  • 不料其不习朝仪，当是福薄 - Suggests that Fu Siye's lack of familiarity with court etiquette might be due to bad luck.
  • 上意亦解 - Indicates that the emperor's displeasure was somewhat alleviated.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 通判 - A local administrative official, often serving as a deputy to the governor.
  • 同知 - A local administrative official, often serving as an associate to the governor.
  • 南安府 - A prefecture in southern China.
  • 阿公 - A respectful term for an older man or a family elder.
  • 河南 - A province in northern China.
  • 华 - A personal name, likely referring to a family member or close associate.
  • 满载而归 - A phrase indicating a successful return, often with wealth or honors.
  • 张荣 - A personal name, likely referring to a family member or close associate.
  • 二千金 - 2,000 taels of gold, a significant sum of money.
  • 小康 - A term indicating a comfortable or moderately prosperous life.
  • 傅四爷 - A respectful term for an older man or a family elder, often used in formal settings.
  • 吏部 - The Ministry of Personnel, responsible for appointments and promotions.
  • 果毅公 - A title of nobility, indicating a high-ranking official.
  • 讷亲 - A personal name, likely referring to a high-ranking official.
  • 光明殿 - A hall in the imperial palace where the emperor would meet officials.
  • 朝仪 - Court etiquette and formal procedures.
  • 福薄 - A term indicating bad luck or a lack of fortune.

Proper Nouns

  • 通判 - A title of a local administrative official.
  • 同知 - A title of a local administrative official.
  • 南安府 - A prefecture in southern China.
  • 阿公 - A respectful term for an older man or a family elder.
  • 河南 - A province in northern China.
  • 华 - A personal name.
  • 张荣 - A personal name.
  • 傅四爷 - A respectful term for an older man or a family elder.
  • 吏部 - The Ministry of Personnel.
  • 果毅公 - A title of nobility.
  • 讷亲 - A personal name.
  • 光明殿 - A hall in the imperial palace.
  • 朝仪 - Court etiquette and formal procedures.
  • 福薄 - A term indicating bad luck or a lack of fortune.

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 未几 - Classical expression meaning 'not long after' or 'soon.'
  • 保举引见 - Refers to the practice of recommending someone for an audience with a superior.
  • 失仪 - Means 'to lose etiquette' or 'to behave improperly.'
  • 千万留神 - Colloquial expression meaning 'be very careful.'
  • 蹈前辙 - Means 'to repeat the same mistake' or 'to fall into the same trap.'
  • 致伤我脸 - Colloquial expression meaning 'to bring shame to me.'
  • 唯唯 - Classical expression of agreement or compliance, often translated as 'yes, yes' or 'I understand.'
  • 履历 - Refers to a person's record or resume.
  • 踉跄涕泣 - Means 'stumbling and weeping.'
  • 背履历 - Means 'to recite one's resume' or 'to state one's qualifications.'
  • 红袍大人 - Refers to a high-ranking official in red robes, a symbol of authority.
  • 直冲而进 - Means 'to charge in directly.'
  • 不受其拦 - Means 'to not be stopped by him.'
  • 手披我颊 - Means 'to slap me across the face.'
  • 提而掷之 - Means 'to pick me up and throw me.'
  • 跌在殿外台坡之下 - Means 'to fall down the steps outside the hall.'
  • 瞻仰天颜 - Means 'to look upon the face of heaven' or 'to see the emperor.'
  • 前生是何冤孽 - Means 'what kind of karmic debt from a past life.'
  • 福薄 - Means 'thin fortune' or 'poor luck.'
  • 求公爷以后亦不必再保举我了 - Means 'I beg my lord to not recommend me again in the future.'
  • 咸为骇异 - Means 'all were greatly surprised.'
  • 遣人扶至车上 - Means 'send someone to carry him to the carriage.'
  • 送归其家 - Means 'send him back to his home.'
  • 随即病发 - Means 'soon after, he fell ill.'
  • 四日而亡 - Means 'he died four days later.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 保举引见 - A common practice in imperial China where officials recommended candidates for appointments.
  • 红袍大人 - Red robes were a symbol of high rank and authority in the imperial court.
  • 殿门 - Refers to the entrance to the imperial palace.
  • 司官 - Refers to officials responsible for specific duties in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 午门外 - The area outside the main gate of the imperial palace.
  • 天颜 - A respectful term for the emperor's face, symbolizing the emperor's presence and authority.
  • 前生 - Refers to a past life in the context of Buddhist or Taoist beliefs.
  • 冤孽 - A term from Buddhist or Taoist beliefs, referring to karmic debts or bad karma.
  • 福薄 - A term from Buddhist or Taoist beliefs, referring to a lack of good fortune or karma.
  • 公爷 - A respectful term for a high-ranking official or noble.
  • 病发 - Refers to the onset of an illness.
  • 四日而亡 - Refers to the rapid progression of illness leading to death.

Proper Nouns

  • 讷公 - A respectful term for a high-ranking official named Nè.
  • 傅 - The name of the person being recommended for the audience.
  • 司官 - A general term for officials responsible for specific duties.
  • 红袍大人 - A general term for a high-ranking official in red robes.
  • 午门外 - A specific location outside the main gate of the imperial palace.
  • 殿门 - A specific location inside the imperial palace.
  • 天颜 - A respectful term for the emperor's face.
  • 前生 - A general term for a past life.
  • 冤孽 - A general term for karmic debt.
  • 福薄 - A general term for poor fortune.
  • 公爷 - A respectful term for a high-ranking official or noble.
  • 病发 - A general term for the onset of an illness.
  • 四日而亡 - A general term for the rapid progression of illness leading to death.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 錢縣丞睢宁县丞钱某权知县事 - Indicates that Qian, the county magistrate, is acting as the magistrate of Suining County. '权知县事' means 'acting magistrate' or 'temporary magistrate.'
  • 有路毙者，相验时地主出钱八千送官，便可结案 - Specific local custom where the landowner pays 8,000 coins to the authorities to close the case of a person found dead on the road.
  • 钱将受矣，见钱用红绳穿系，色甚鲜华 - Describes the money being tied with a red string, a common practice in Chinese culture to signify good luck or auspiciousness.
  • 此钱系逼迫而来，不忍滥受 - Indicates that the money was obtained under duress, and the magistrate felt it would be wrong to accept it.
  • 我决不受此钱，汝速退归此女 - Strong statement by the magistrate, refusing to accept the money and ordering the return of the girl.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 路毙者 - Refers to a person found dead on the road, a common occurrence in traditional Chinese society.
  • 相验 - The process of examining the body to determine the cause of death, often involving local officials.
  • 地主 - The landowner, who in this context is also the person responsible for paying the fee to close the case.
  • 红绳 - A red string, often used in Chinese culture to tie money or other items, symbolizing good fortune or blessings.
  • 逼迫 - Forced or coerced, indicating that the money was given under pressure or duress.
  • 胥役 - Subordinate officials or clerks who assist in administrative tasks, often involved in corrupt practices.

Proper Nouns

  • 钱县丞 - Qian, the county magistrate.
  • 睢宁县丞 - Suining County, the location where the magistrate is acting.
  • 胥役 - Subordinate officials or clerks, not a specific individual.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 无何 - Temporal phrase meaning 'soon' or 'shortly after,' indicating a brief passage of time.
  • 患背疽 - Medical condition, 'back abscess,' which was a serious illness in ancient times.
  • 昏迷于床 - Describes the state of being unconscious and lying in bed.
  • 梦青衣人召至一处 - Dream sequence involving a man in green clothes, often associated with messengers or officials in classical literature.
  • 殿宇巍峨 - Describes a grand and imposing building, often used to depict heavenly or imperial architecture.
  • 上座王者 - Refers to a supreme ruler or deity seated on the throne.
  • 汝大数已尽 - Phrase from the concept of 'life span' or 'fate,' meaning 'your allotted time has come to an end.'
  • 幸有一善事 - '幸' means 'fortunately,' and '善事' refers to a good deed.
  • 足以抵偿 - Means 'sufficient to offset' or 'enough to make up for.'
  • 汝知之乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'do you know this?'
  • 茫然不解 - Describes a state of confusion or lack of understanding.
  • 王首肯之 - '首肯' means 'nodding in agreement,' indicating approval.
  • 送其还魂 - '还魂' means 'revive' or 'bring back to life.'
  • 疽遂霍然 - '霍然' means 'suddenly' or 'immediately,' describing the sudden healing of the abscess.
  • 一心行善 - Means 'to focus on doing good deeds.'
  • 凡赈饥埋棺等事 - Refers to various charitable acts such as providing food for the hungry and burying the dead.
  • 悉捐资为之 - Means 'to fully contribute funds to these causes.'
  • 官果氵存擢同知 - '氵存' is a typo, likely intended to be '升' (shēng), meaning 'promoted.' '同知' is a government position, equivalent to a deputy magistrate.
  • 一纪之期已满 - '一纪' refers to a period of twelve years, a common unit of time in ancient Chinese.
  • 家人将理后事 - Means 'family members are preparing for funeral arrangements.'
  • 意尚迟疑 - Describes a state of hesitation or uncertainty.
  • 慰钱曰 - Means 'comforted him by saying.'
  • 公前有一善 - Refers to a previous good deed.
  • 寿尚可延 - Means 'life can still be extended.'
  • 年来善行甚多 - Means 'many good deeds have been done in recent years.'
  • 冥中不再为益算乎 - '冥中' refers to the underworld or afterlife, and '益算' means 'additional life span.'
  • 昔之善无所为而为之也 - Means 'the good deeds of the past were done without any ulterior motive.'
  • 阴间重我 - Means 'the underworld values me.'
  • 今之善，有所为而为之也 - Means 'the good deeds of today are done with a specific purpose.'
  • 恐阴间未心重我 - Means 'I fear the underworld does not value me as much.'
  • 此番数尽 - Means 'this time, your fate has come to an end.'
  • 断不能逃 - Means 'it is impossible to escape.'
  • 或者有心为善，终与有心为恶者不同 - Means 'perhaps doing good with a heart of goodness is different from doing evil with a heart of malice.'
  • 或者他生其有报乎 - Means 'perhaps there will be a reward in a future life.'
  • 不数日，疽溃而卒 - Means 'a few days later, the abscess burst, and he died.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 背疽 - A serious medical condition that was often fatal in ancient times.
  • 青衣人 - Often refers to messengers or officials in classical literature, sometimes associated with divine or supernatural beings.
  • 殿宇巍峨 - Grand architecture, often used to describe heavenly or imperial settings.
  • 上座王者 - A supreme ruler or deity, often associated with the afterlife or divine judgment.
  • 大数已尽 - A concept from Chinese cosmology and philosophy, referring to the predetermined lifespan of a person.
  • 善事 - Good deeds, which were highly valued in Confucian and Taoist thought.
  • 阴间 - The underworld or afterlife, a common theme in Chinese folklore and religious beliefs.
  • 冥中 - Another term for the underworld, emphasizing the realm of spirits and deities.
  • 同知 - A government position, equivalent to a deputy magistrate, indicating a moderate level of official rank.
  • 一纪 - A period of twelve years, a common unit of time in ancient Chinese.
  • 家人将理后事 - Preparing for funeral arrangements, a significant cultural practice in ancient China.
  • 昔之善无所为而为之也 - Emphasizes the purity of past good deeds, a key concept in moral philosophy.
  • 今之善，有所为而为之也 - Highlights the difference between selfless and self-serving actions.
  • 他生 - A future life, reflecting the belief in reincarnation or the afterlife.

Proper Nouns

  • 钱 - The name of the main character, a common name in Chinese.
  • 青衣人 - A messenger or official in green clothes, often associated with divine or supernatural beings.
  • 上座王者 - A supreme ruler or deity, often associated with the afterlife or divine judgment.
  • 判官 - A judge or official in the underworld, often associated with divine or supernatural beings.
  • 同知 - A government position, equivalent to a deputy magistrate.
  • 阴间 - The underworld or afterlife, a common theme in Chinese folklore and religious beliefs.
  • 冥中 - Another term for the underworld, emphasizing the realm of spirits and deities.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 扶乩 - A form of divination using a pendulum or similar device to communicate with spirits.
  • 女仙降坛 - Refers to a female deity descending to the altar.
  • 涛声如梦恨如烟 - Poetic expression meaning 'the sound of waves is like a dream, and resentment is like smoke,' evoking a sense of melancholy and nostalgia.
  • 泉台一去千余载 - Literally means 'once gone to the Springs of the Dead for more than a thousand years,' referring to death and the afterlife.
  • 只抵相思半日眠 - Means 'only worth a half-day's sleep of longing,' expressing the intensity of love and loss.
  • 隔世逢鱼水之交 - Means 'meeting an intimate friendship across lifetimes,' emphasizing the deep bond between the speaker and the listener.
  • 红泪千行 - Refers to 'thousands of rows of red tears,' symbolizing intense sorrow.
  • 不昧素心一点 - Means 'not forgetting a single pure heart,' indicating the persistence of true feelings.
  • 河山之阻 - Refers to 'the obstacle of mountains and rivers,' symbolizing physical and emotional distance.
  • 显晦之攸殊 - Means 'the difference between being visible and hidden,' highlighting the contrast between life and death.
  • 精诚之易合 - Means 'the ease of coming together through sincerity and faith,' suggesting that true feelings can transcend physical barriers.
  • 窗明风露冷 - Describes a scene where the window is bright and the wind and dew are cold, setting a mood of loneliness and longing.
  • 梦美魂酣时觅我 - Means 'seek me during beautiful dreams and deep sleep,' indicating the desire to meet in dreams.
  • 不呼名氏 - Means 'not calling out names,' implying a subtle and respectful approach to communication.
  • 惟续情缘 - Means 'only continuing the emotional bond,' emphasizing the importance of maintaining the relationship.
  • 讵崇殃之敢作 - Means 'how dare one cause harm,' expressing the assurance that no harm will come from the interaction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 樵川杨苛锄与金陵徐沧浔 - Refers to specific individuals from different regions, indicating a cross-regional interaction.
  • 女仙降坛 - A common theme in Chinese folklore and literature, where deities or spirits communicate with humans.
  • 涛神侍从 - Refers to attendants of the wave god, suggesting a divine or supernatural context.
  • 符使招携 - Refers to the use of talismans or magical symbols to summon or communicate with spirits.
  • 鱼水之交 - A metaphor for a close and harmonious relationship, often used in Chinese literature.
  • 红泪 - A traditional symbol of intense sorrow and emotional pain.
  • 显晦 - Refers to the contrast between being visible and hidden, often used in philosophical and literary contexts.
  • 精诚 - Refers to sincerity and faith, important virtues in Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 樵川杨苛锄 - A specific individual from a region called Qiuchuan.
  • 金陵徐沧浔 - A specific individual from Nanjing.
  • 王氏小筠 - The name of the female deity, indicating her family name and personal name.

[208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 越日 - Classical expression meaning 'the next day.'
  • 降乩 - Refers to a mediumistic session in which a spirit communicates through a medium.
  • 赤甲风头雨似尘 - Poetic expression describing a storm with red clouds and rain like dust.
  • 天风吹送步虚人 - Poetic expression suggesting that the wind carries a spirit or divine being.
  • 请君试采梅花嗅 - Poetic invitation to smell the plum blossoms.
  • 老却琼香树树春 - Poetic expression suggesting that the plum trees will remain fragrant and youthful.
  • 露里夭桃风外柳 - Poetic description of dew-covered peach blossoms and wind-blown willows.
  • 昨宵几执纤纤手 - Poetic expression suggesting that the hands were held last night.
  • 千秋无尽是相思 - Poetic expression suggesting eternal longing.
  • 绿卿又到君知否 - Poetic expression suggesting that the spirit of '绿卿' has returned, and the question is whether the addressee knows.
  • 珍重 - Classical expression meaning 'take care' or 'farewell.'
  • 合卺 - Classical term for the wedding ceremony.
  • 头眩疾 - Refers to a dizziness or vertigo illness.
  • 辄仆地不知人事 - Means 'immediately falls to the ground and loses consciousness.'
  • 依稀若尺许小儿 - Vague description of a small child-like figure.
  • 羸瘦 - Means 'thin and weak.'
  • 延术士遣之 - Means 'invited a magician to exorcise it.'
  • 砉刂然 - Describes a sharp, sudden sound.
  • 木偶小儿 - Refers to a wooden puppet child.
  • 穿红衣，颈缠红丝 - Describes a puppet child wearing a red robe and having a red thread around its neck.
  • 妖遂绝 - Means 'the spirit was finally expelled.'
  • 入赘 - Refers to a man moving into his wife's family home.
  • 修葺房宇 - Means 'repairing the house.'
  • 求而不遂 - Means 'requests that were not fulfilled.'
  • 压魁术 - Refers to a magical or supernatural technique.
  • 病骨支离 - Means 'sickly and weak.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 降乩 - A mediumistic practice common in Chinese folk religion, where a spirit communicates through a medium.
  • 赤甲风头雨似尘 - A poetic description that could be inspired by natural phenomena or mythological imagery.
  • 天风吹送步虚人 - A poetic expression that could be inspired by Daoist or Buddhist spiritual imagery.
  • 绿卿 - A name that could refer to a spirit or a historical figure, but its exact identity is unclear without further context.
  • 合卺 - A traditional Chinese wedding ceremony where the couple drinks from a shared wine vessel.
  • 入赘 - A traditional Chinese custom where a man moves into his wife's family home, often practiced in certain regions or families.
  • 压魁术 - A term that suggests a form of magic or supernatural technique, possibly related to folk beliefs or practices.

Proper Nouns

  • 高念东 - The name of a high-ranking official, likely a reference to a historical figure.
  • 明经 - A title or name used to refer to the patient.
  • 勒勒 - The name of a small figure or spirit, possibly a child.
  • 绿卿 - The name of a spirit or a historical figure, but its exact identity is unclear without further context.
  • 某匠 - A reference to a local craftsman, whose name is not specified.

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 扶小仆至亭 - Literally 'supported by a small servant to the pavilion,' where '小仆' likely refers to a young servant or attendant.
  • 即命仆归内室取茶具 - 'Immediately ordered the servant to return to the inner chamber to fetch the tea set.'
  • 冉冉自墙而下 - Describes the girl descending the wall in a graceful manner, similar to floating or drifting.
  • 知君渴，愿以奉君 - A polite expression meaning 'knowing you are thirsty, I wish to offer you this.'
  • 君领此，妾当去耳 - A formal statement indicating that the speaker will leave once the recipient accepts the tea.
  • 惟一桑叶贮一撮土 - A mysterious ending where the tea cup contains only a mulberry leaf holding a pinch of soil, suggesting an enigmatic transformation or disappearance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 明经 - Refers to a scholar or a person knowledgeable in Confucian classics, often a title given to those who passed the imperial examinations.
  • 邻旧有女，笄而美 - '邻旧' refers to a neighbor from the past, and '笄' indicates that the girl is of marriageable age, having performed the coming-of-age ceremony for women.
  • 旧病未复 - Suggests a previous illness that has not fully recovered, possibly alluding to a common theme in classical literature where characters are weakened or vulnerable.
  • 慧心 - Refers to a clever or insightful mind, often used to describe someone with a sharp intellect or a deep understanding of subtle matters.
  • 举座冷气逼人 - Describes an eerie, chilling atmosphere that affects everyone present, hinting at supernatural or ghostly elements.

Proper Nouns

  • 明经 - A title for a scholar or a person knowledgeable in Confucian classics.
  • 邻旧 - Refers to a neighbor from the past, though no specific name is provided.
  • 女 - The girl, whose identity remains unclear, but she is referred to as '女' (female) throughout the text.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 明经一日梦与夫人为欢 - Scholar Mingjing dreamed of being intimate with a woman.
  • 醒觉，乃即女 - Upon waking, he realized it was a woman.
  • 明经知为其术所幻 - Mingjing knew it was a trick of her art.
  • 然欲强留之，女遽揽衣下床，大笑而去 - However, he wanted to keep her, but the woman quickly put on her clothes, got off the bed, and laughed loudly before leaving.
  • 摄其衣，如纸瑟瑟有声 - He pulled her clothes, and they rustled like paper.
  • 后明经得导引之法，女遂绝迹 - Later, Mingjing learned the method of guidance, and the woman then disappeared completely.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 明经 - Refers to a scholar or literatus, often associated with the imperial examinations in ancient China.
  • 导引之法 - A method of guidance or control, possibly referring to a form of spiritual or physical exercise.
  • 雷击两妇活一儿 - Describes a supernatural event where two women were struck by lightning, and one child survived. This could be a reference to a myth or legend about divine intervention.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '明经一日梦与夫人为欢，醒觉，乃即女，明经知为其术所幻' describes a dream and the subsequent realization that it was an illusion.
  • The clause '然欲强留之，女遽揽衣下床，大笑而去' describes the attempt to keep the woman and her reaction.
  • The phrase '摄其衣，如纸瑟瑟有声' describes the act of pulling the woman's clothes and the resulting sound.
  • The final clause '后明经得导引之法，女遂绝迹' describes the outcome after the scholar learned the method of guidance.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 三朝复请稳婆洗儿 - Traditional Chinese custom where a midwife performs a ritual bath for the newborn three days after birth.
  • 洗儿时暗以针刺儿脐心致死 - Secretly stabbing the child's navel with a needle during the washing ritual, causing its death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三朝 - Refers to the third day after a child's birth, a significant day in traditional Chinese culture.

  • 然而，室中常闻鬼啾啾哭声 - Means 'however, in the room, they often heard ghostly cries,' indicating the supernatural consequences.

  • 未几，次妇子亦病 - Means 'not long after, the younger wife's son also fell ill,' continuing the pattern of retribution.
  • 怖甚，嘱夫环守之 - Means 'terrified, she asked her husband to guard him around the clock,' indicating her fear and desperation.
  • 夜二鼓，忽阴风袭人 - Means 'at two drums at night, suddenly a cold wind blew,' setting the scene for the supernatural event.
  • 门帘豁然启，见一卒赤发蓝面，齿长数寸，手执钢叉，直入床前攫其子去 - Means 'the door curtain suddenly opened, and they saw a soldier with red hair and blue face, long teeth, and a steel fork, who grabbed the child and took him away,' describing the ghostly figure.

  • 暇日欲绘一小像 - The desire to paint a portrait reflects the appreciation for art and personal expression in classical Chinese culture.

  • 甚肖 - The emphasis on a lifelike portrait underscores the importance of realism in classical Chinese art.
  • 公长谈佛法 - The gentleman's interest in Buddhism reflects the widespread influence of Buddhist philosophy in Chinese society.
  • 为公写真，甚肖 - The act of painting a portrait with high fidelity is a significant cultural practice, often associated with the elite.
  • 此吾前生矣 - The reference to a past life in the context of reincarnation is a common theme in Buddhist and Taoist thought.
  • 曩吾督师滇中 - The mention of Yunnan as a military command reflects the geographical and political context of the time.
  • 适额驸色布腾珠尔布纳病剧 - The reference to a prince consort's illness and subsequent revival is a dramatic element that adds to the narrative's emotional impact.
  • 绝而复苏 - The concept of death and revival is a powerful motif in classical Chinese literature, often symbolizing rebirth or transformation.
  • 趣左右，邀我至榻前 - The urgency and respect shown in the narrative reflect the social norms and relationships of the time.
  • 顷至一山，长松插天，苍翠四匝 - The detailed description of the mountain setting evokes a sense of natural beauty and spiritual significance.
  • 中有石洞，列古罗汉数尊 - The presence of arhats in a stone cave suggests a sacred or mystical place, often associated with Buddhist temples.
  • 旁设蒲团，虚其坐 - The arrangement of mats and an empty seat reflects the Buddhist practice of meditation and reverence.
  • 一罗汉指示曰 - The arhat's guidance adds a spiritual dimension to the narrative, emphasizing the moral and ethical lessons.
  • 此阿某旧居也 - The reference to a former residence in the context of reincarnation ties the narrative to Buddhist beliefs.
  • 以误杀一金童谪人间 - The punishment for killing a golden child is a metaphorical expression of the consequences of one's actions in a karmic framework.

  • 名公巨卿咏殆遍 - The widespread appreciation of the painting by prominent officials highlights its cultural and artistic value.
  • 而缪生由此以传神名日下 - Mr. Miao's fame for capturing the spirit in his art reflects the high regard for artistic skill in classical Chinese culture.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 魍魉 - A term referring to a type of ghost or spirit in Chinese folklore. It is often used to describe a malevolent or supernatural being. In this context, it is personified as a spirit that can influence human fate.
  • 九品官 - A reference to the ancient Chinese civil service rank system, where officials were divided into nine grades. A 'nine-grade official' would be a relatively high-ranking official.
  • 田三千亩 - Three thousand acres of farmland. In ancient China, land ownership was a major indicator of wealth and social status.
  • 屋九椽 - Nine rafters in a house. In classical Chinese, the number of rafters in a house could indicate its size and status. A house with nine rafters would be a fairly large and prosperous home.
  • 男子二人 - Two male sons. This is a traditional indicator of family prosperity and continuity.
  • 巡检 - A local government official in ancient China, responsible for inspecting and maintaining order in a region. The *xunjian* was a lower-level official, often stationed in a county or district.
  • 科甲 - Refers to the imperial examination system, particularly the *jinshi* (進士) degree, which was the highest level of the civil service examination. To 'pass the *kejia*' meant to achieve academic and official success.
  • 外父 - A term used for a father-in-law, meaning 'the father of one's wife.'

Proper Nouns

  • 魍魉山阴高进士之父某翁 - A composite proper noun referring to 'Old Master Gao, the father of a scholar from Shanyin who was a magistrate (jinshi).'
  • 山阴 - A district in present-day Shaoxing, Zhejiang Province. In classical Chinese, it is often used to denote a person from Shanyin.
  • 高进士 - 'Master Gao, the scholar-official' – '进士' refers to someone who passed the highest level of the imperial examination.
  • 某翁 - 'Old Master' or 'a certain old man' – a euphemistic or respectful term for an unnamed elderly man.
  • 冯村人姓冯者 - A man from a village named Feng, whose surname is Feng. This is a bit of a tautology, but it is used to emphasize the identity of the person.
  • 山西巡检 - A local official in the Shanxi province, as mentioned earlier.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生嫂诣生室大言责曰 - The wife of the scholar went to his room and loudly reprimanded him. *诣* means 'to visit' or 'to go to'; *大言责曰* is a compound expression meaning 'to speak loudly in reprimand' or 'to scold loudly.'
  • 妖狐岂无羞耻 - How can this demon fox have no shame? *妖狐* is 'demon fox,' a common term in Chinese folklore for a fox spirit with supernatural powers. *岂无羞耻* is a rhetorical question meaning 'how can it not feel shame?'
  • 强欲夺人婿 - Trying to forcefully take away someone's son-in-law. *强欲* means 'forcibly desire' or 'try to take by force'; *夺人婿* is 'take someone's husband' or 'take away someone's son-in-law.'
  • 吾家小叔幼已订婚某室 - My younger brother was already betrothed to a certain family when he was young. *小叔* refers to 'younger brother' or 'younger male sibling'; *订婚* is 'betrothed' or 'engaged'; *某室* is a euphemistic way of referring to a family or household, often used when the name is unknown or omitted.
  • 嫡庶 - A term from Confucian family law, referring to *嫡* (legitimate, first-born son of the main wife) and *庶* (illegitimate, born to a concubine or妾). It denotes the distinction between legitimate and illegitimate offspring in traditional Chinese society.
  • 泣谓生曰 - Cried and said to the scholar. *泣* is 'to weep'; *谓* is 'to say' or 'to address.'
  • 两相唏嘘于枕畔 - They both wept and sighed by the bedside. *唏嘘* is a compound term for weeping and sighing, often used to express emotional distress or sorrow.
  • 闻鸡唱，遂下榻而没 - Upon hearing the rooster crow, he descended from the bed and vanished. *闻鸡唱* is a reference to the ancient practice of waking at dawn, often associated with the legend of Zhuge Liang or the 'chicken crowing' as a signal for the day to begin. *下榻* means 'to get out of bed'; *没* means 'to vanish' or 'to disappear.'
  • 工词律，善拳棒 - Skilled in poetry and meter, and adept at boxing and martial arts. *工词律* refers to being skilled in poetic composition and meter; *善拳棒* refers to being skilled in martial arts, particularly boxing and stick-fighting.
  • 惜传者未记 - Unfortunately, those who passed it on did not record it. *传者* means 'those who transmitted it' or 'the people who passed it on'; *未记* is 'did not record.'
  • 新安洪介亭所说 - As said by Hong Jieting of Xin’an. *新安* is a historical county in present-day Anhui Province; *洪介亭* is the name of a person, likely a scholar or recorder of the tale.
  • 李亦自言不讳 - Li also spoke of it without hesitation. *自言不讳* means 'to speak openly' or 'to confess without reservation.'
  • 粤东亢旱 - Yue Dong suffered from a severe drought. *粤东* refers to the eastern part of the region of Guangdong (Guangdong is often called *Yue* in classical Chinese); *亢旱* means 'severe drought.'
  • 制军孙公祷雨无验 - Governor-General Sun prayed for rain, but it did not work. *制军* is an honorific title for a governor-general or high-ranking military official; *祷雨* is 'to pray for rain'; *无验* means 'no effect' or 'no success.'
  • 看仙童 - To watch the divine boy. *仙童* is 'immortal boy' or 'divine child,' often used in folktales to describe a child with supernatural abilities.
  • 哄动乡邻 - Caused a commotion among the neighbors. *哄动* is 'to cause a commotion'; *乡邻* is 'village neighbors' or 'neighbors in the village.'
  • 童但俯笑不言 - The child merely smiled and said nothing. *但* is 'only' or 'merely'; *俯笑* is 'smiled down' or 'smiled while looking down'; *不言* is 'did not speak.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妖狐 - Demon fox is a common motif in Chinese folklore and literature, often representing a shapeshifting spirit or a supernatural being with both malevolent and seductive traits. The term *狐* (fox) is often associated with *狐仙* (fox spirit), a supernatural being capable of transformation and often depicted as a seductress or trickster.
  • 嫡庶 - This term reflects the Confucian family structure in traditional Chinese society, where the *嫡* (legitimate) child of the main wife was considered superior to the *庶* (illegitimate) children of concubines. This distinction was crucial in inheritance, status, and social hierarchy.
  • 新安洪介亭 - *Xin’an* was a historical county in southern Anhui, known for its literary and scholarly tradition. *Hong Jieting* is likely a historical figure or compiler of tales, possibly a scholar or local official who recorded this story.
  • 粤东 - Refers to the eastern region of Guangdong Province, historically known as *Yue* in classical Chinese. This region was part of the Lingnan area, which had a distinct cultural identity.
  • 制军 - A title for a high-ranking military official, often used for governors or regional commanders in the Qing dynasty. *制军孙公* would be 'Governor-General Sun.'
  • 仙童 - 'Divine boy' or 'immortal child' is a common figure in Chinese mythology, often associated with supernatural abilities or divine intervention. In this context, the child is being described as having extraordinary powers, possibly linked to the supernatural or divine.
  • 制军孙公祷雨无验 - This alludes to the traditional practice of officials performing rituals or prayers for rain during droughts, which was common in Chinese society, especially during the imperial era.

  • 其母以干冬菜蒸肉脯食之 - Describes the mother cooking dried winter vegetables with meat as a meal for the boys.
  • 时正暑，儿食后洗浴，久之不出，怪而视之 - Narrates the boys bathing after eating during the hot summer, then not emerging from the bath for a long time, prompting their family to investigate.
  • 则盆中惟有血水，骨肉皆销 - Reveals that only blood water remains in the tub, with the boys' flesh and bones dissolved.

  • 然考《遵生书》云 - Introduces a reference to an ancient text, *The Book of Nurturing Life*.

  • ○蘭渚山北來大仙會稽蘭渚山有蘭亭道院焉 - Classical style of introducing a location and its significance; the ○ symbol denotes the beginning of a section or story. The phrase 有蘭亭道院焉 means 'has the Lan Ting Dao Yuan (Daoist monastery).'
  • 北來大仙 - A term for 'great immortal who came from the north.'

  • 喪資本，貧窭不能自給，且病 - Compound clause describing multiple conditions: 傷資本 – 'lost his capital,' 貧窭 – 'poor and destitute,' 不能自給 – 'could not support himself,' and 且病 – 'and was ill.'
  • 居廢寺中 - 'Lived in a ruined temple.'

  • 遂去 - 'Then she left.'
  • 如是數月，枕席諧暢，情好日篤 - 'Like this, for several months, their relationship was harmonious in bedchamber and affection grew daily.' 枕席諧暢 – 'harmonious in the bedchamber,' 情好日篤 – 'affection deepened day by day.'
  • 金钏稍赎资斧 - 'Used the gold bracelet to gradually redeem capital.' 资斧 is a classical term for funds or capital.

  • 購新居，料其家事 - 'Bought a new home, managed her household affairs.'
  • 日致金银珠宝之物，不下巨万 - 'Daily brought gold, silver, jewels, and other valuables, amounting to no less than millions.' 日致 – 'daily brought,' 不下巨万 – 'not less than millions' or 'amounting to millions.'
